summary: Crisp soundboard recording of a scorching spring ’77 show, highlighted by a blistering Scarlet Fire, over‑the‑top Estimated Prophet and a powerhouse Tennessee Jed.
review: The remastered soundboard capture is exceptionally clear—deep bass, crisp drums and full‑range keyboards let every nuance shine. Musically the night is a tour‑de‑force: Jerry’s vocal intensity on Jack Straw, Keith’s soaring solo on They Love Each Other, an epic Estimated Prophet/He’s Gone stretch, and a fiery Scarlet Fire that kept the audience buzzing. Overall the performance is judged as one of the strongest of the 1977 spring leg.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer scores (mostly 4‑5 stars) and enthusiastic language about both the music and the crystal‑clear soundboard make this an outstanding show with excellent audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, full‑range mix; deep bass and clear cymbal detail. Minor dead‑air cuts noted but overall very clean after remastering.

standout_songs: Saint Stephen > Not Fade Away > Saint Stephen
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds with tight jams that keep momentum, while the second set flows like an odyssey—Samson > Bertha Women into a flawless Estimated Prophet/He’s Gone stretch and a seamless Saint Stephen‑Not Fade Away suite.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocally “all in” on Jack Straw; fiery guitar work on Tennessee Jed, pinch‑harmonic runs, strong vocals throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s solo on They Love Each Other praised as “awesome”; keyboards described as crisp and full, with tasteful mutron/envelope filter textures on Scarlet Fire.
band_member_comments.Drums: Deep bass and precise cymbal detail noted; drum breaks described as concise and “blistering” during Too.
